bug_report.summary: Word/character count drops to only count main text when inline footnote content is present (stats inconsistent with visible content)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Type: `Example text ^[Inline footnote with *italic*, **bold**, and a link to [Wikipedia](https://www.wikipedia.org/).]`
Observe the word/character counts in the UI (top-right and status bar).
expected_behaviors: Word/character counts should include the typed content (or at minimum remain consistent with prior behavior for the same input).
Counts should correspond to the visible document content.
actual_behaviors: Counts show only ~2 words / ~17 characters (appearing to count only “Example text”), despite the inline footnote content being present/visible in the editor.

bug_report.summary: Markdown link inside inline footnote loses its destination/ceases to be a link after saving footnote (Shift+Return)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Type: `Example text ^[Inline footnote with *italic*, **bold**, and a link to [Wikipedia](https://www.wikipedia.org/).]`
Enter inline footnote edit mode, append ` Added.` to the end, then press Shift+Return to save.
Hover or otherwise inspect the `Wikipedia` link inside the footnote content.
expected_behaviors: The `Wikipedia` text remains a Markdown link with its destination preserved (https://www.wikipedia.org/), consistent with the typed source.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the URL destination portion disappears from the editor rendering and the `Wikipedia` text no longer behaves as a link (no link preview tooltip/URL shown).

bug_report.summary: Markdown preview heading marker (#) rendering changes/looks corrupted near “Helpful Links” heading
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app and dismiss the setup wizard/update dialog to reach the main interface with the bundled ‘Split View / Helpful Links’ content visible.
Observe the Markdown preview/reading pane where the “Helpful Links” H1 heading is shown (the green # heading marker to the left of the title).
expected_behaviors: Heading marker/iconography (#) should render consistently (same shape/position) as before the change.
No visible corruption/duplication/clipping of the heading marker.
actual_behaviors: The green # heading marker next to “Helpful Links” renders differently than before (visual change within the marker area), suggesting a regression in general Markdown preview styling/rendering unrelated to footnotes.

bug_report.summary: Word/character count changes significantly for same document content when editing multi-block footnotes
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Enter the content from the test scenario, including a multi-block footnote definition with a Tab-indented continuation paragraph and link.
Observe the word/character counters (top-right and/or status bar) for the active document.
expected_behaviors: Word and character counts should be consistent for the same document text and should not change as a side effect of footnote rendering refactors (unless explicitly intended/communicated).
actual_behaviors: Counters differ substantially between builds for the same visible text (e.g., 9 words/55 characters vs 22 words/129 characters), suggesting a change in counting logic tied to the footnote parsing/rendering refactor.

bug_report.summary: Footnote hover preview omits list items / does not render full multi-block footnote body
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Type the scenario content including a footnote reference and a footnote definition whose body contains a Tab-indented continuation paragraph and Tab-indented list items (“- item one”, “- item two”).
Hover the mouse over the `[^note]` reference in the editor.
expected_behaviors: Hover preview should render the complete footnote definition content, including subsequent blocks such as the Tab-indented list items, consistent with ‘richer content inside footnote definition bodies’.
Preview should reflect multi-block parsing (paragraph + list) rather than only the first part.
actual_behaviors: The hover preview popover shows only the first paragraph and the continuation paragraph text, but the list items under the footnote definition are not shown/rendered in the preview.

bug_report.summary: Footnote hover preview incorrectly includes following non-indented paragraph (footnote continuation does not terminate)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Enter exactly:
A ref [^c].

[^c]: First line of footnote.
    Continuation line that should still be in the footnote.

This paragraph is not indented and must not be part of the footnote.
Hover the mouse over the `[^c]` reference in the editor/preview.
expected_behaviors: The hover preview should show only the footnote definition content (the first line and the indented continuation).
The following non-indented paragraph should not appear in the footnote preview.
actual_behaviors: The hover preview popover includes the subsequent non-indented paragraph (“This paragraph is not indented…”) as if it were part of the footnote definition.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Footnote hover preview formatting regresses: line breaks collapsed and text rendered with odd spacing
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Enter a footnote definition with an indented continuation line (as in the test scenario).
Hover the mouse over the `[^c]` reference.
expected_behaviors: The hover preview should preserve intended line structure (e.g., show the continuation on its own line/paragraph as authored).
Text in the preview should use consistent normal styling without abnormal spacing/monospace-like rendering.
actual_behaviors: The first footnote line and the continuation line are collapsed into a single line in the popover (missing the expected line break).
The subsequent sentence in the popover is displayed with abnormal spacing/formatting (appears visually different from normal paragraph text).

bug_report.summary: Unexpected warning/alert indicator appears in editor/preview pane after pressing Enter in footnote continuation line
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Type the scenario content with a footnote definition and an indented continuation line.
Click at the end of the indented continuation line.
Press Enter (before typing the 4 spaces for the next indented blank line).
expected_behaviors: No warning/alert indicator should appear merely from inserting a newline while editing a footnote definition (behavior should match previous build).
actual_behaviors: After the change, a warning/alert triangle icon appears near the lower-right pane/status area immediately after pressing Enter (bbox ~[822,706]-[840,722]); this indicator is not shown in the before-build at the same step.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Status bar caret position/offset and character count diverge significantly after the same keystrokes while editing footnote continuation (e.g., 76 vs 58 chars)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.
Enter the scenario content (with a footnote definition and an indented continuation line).
Place the caret at the end of the indented continuation line.
Press Enter, type 4 spaces on the blank line, then press Enter again (as in the scenario).
Observe the bottom status bar of the active (lower-right) pane.
expected_behaviors: After identical text and identical keystrokes, the status bar should report consistent caret position (line:column/offset) and character count between builds.
actual_behaviors: For the same step, the status bar values differ between builds (e.g., the character count shows 76 in the before-build vs 58 in the after-build, and the caret position/offset also differs).

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Indented continuation after an indented blank line renders as a code block in footnote preview
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Enter:
A ref [^c].

[^c]: First line of footnote.
    Continuation line that should still be in the footnote.

This paragraph is not indented and must not be part of the footnote.
Place cursor at end of the indented continuation line, press Enter.
Type 4 spaces on the new blank line, press Enter again.
Type: `    Indented line after an indented blank line should still be part of the footnote.`
View the rendered preview/reading view (or keep the pane in Rendering: Preview).
expected_behaviors: The newly added indented line should be treated as normal footnote body content (a continued paragraph/block inside the footnote), not rendered as a code block.
actual_behaviors: The new indented line is rendered with code-block styling (grey/monospace block) in the footnote preview, indicating it is being parsed as a code block rather than normal footnote content.

bug_report.summary: Word/character counts become incorrect or fail to update after adding indented blank line + indented continuation to footnote
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document and enter the scenario text.
Edit the footnote by inserting an indented blank line and then an indented continuation line (as described in the test steps).
Observe the bottom status bar stats for the active pane (words/characters).
expected_behaviors: After adding the extra indented continuation sentence, the word/character counts should increase accordingly and remain consistent across builds for the same visible content.
actual_behaviors: Counts differ drastically between builds for the same step: before-change shows higher totals (e.g., ~42 words / ~260 characters) while after-change still shows the earlier totals (~28 words / ~158 characters), suggesting the stats are incorrect or not updating after this edit.

bug_report.summary: Word and character counts change for the same typed invalid footnote patterns (stats regression)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document

Type exactly:
Text [^bad id]
Text [^bad^]
Text [^[bad]]
Text [^]
Observe the word/character counts in the UI (top-right indicator and/or bottom status bar for the active editor pane)
expected_behaviors: Document statistics (word count and character count) should be consistent with previous behavior for the same input text (no unexpected counting rule changes introduced by the footnote refactor)
actual_behaviors: After the change, the same input yields different stats than before (e.g., word count and character count increase: top-right shows 6→8 words; status bar shows 34→50 characters)

bug_report.summary: Rendering/formatting of the typed invalid footnote patterns block differs between builds
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document

Type exactly:
Text [^bad id]
Text [^bad^]
Text [^[bad]]
Text [^]
Look at the rendered appearance of these four lines in the editor pane (the block containing the four lines)
expected_behaviors: Invalid footnote patterns should remain plain text and render consistently (no unexpected styling/formatting changes between builds)
actual_behaviors: The visual rendering of the four-line block differs between before/after builds (diff region bbox ~[896,535,1015,643]), indicating a formatting/styling change unrelated to the stated intent

bug_report.summary: Inline formatting popover (shown on text selection) renders differently between builds (buttons/glyphs/controls change)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document

Type exactly:
Text [^bad id]
Text [^bad^]
Text [^[bad]]
Text [^]
Triple-click the first line to select it (so the inline formatting popover appears)
Compare the inline formatting popover’s buttons/icons
expected_behaviors: The inline formatting popover should appear with the same set of buttons/icons and consistent rendering as before the change.
actual_behaviors: The inline formatting popover’s controls render differently between before/after builds (multiple button regions differ; see diff bboxes ~[899,601,914,620], [929,600,944,620], [958,600,975,621], [986,599,1006,622], [1016,601,1036,620], [1048,602,1065,619] and surrounding popover area ~[882,535,1232,602]).

bug_report.summary: Editor text reflows differently after change: footnote-definition line wraps at a different point ("here." ends up alone on a new line)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Type:
Bad separation ref [^p]

[^p]: This should not be parsed as a footnote definition here.
Ensure the editor pane width is similar to the screenshots (split view as shown).
Observe how the second line (the [^p]: definition line) wraps in the editor.
expected_behaviors: Text wrapping in the editor should remain consistent unless a deliberate editor font/style/layout change was intended.
The footnote definition line should not reflow unexpectedly compared to the previous build.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the footnote definition line wraps differently, with the final word “here.” moving to its own line (visible in the after-change screenshot), suggesting an unintended style/font metric/layout shift in the editor.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Footnote hover popup no longer shows the footnote label/marker (“p”) at start of popup content
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Type:
Bad separation ref [^p]

[^p]: This should not be parsed as a footnote definition here.
Hover the mouse over the `[^p]` reference in the first line.
expected_behaviors: Hover popup should consistently include the footnote identifier/marker (e.g., `p`) or otherwise present the same structured header information as in the previous build (unless explicitly changed).
actual_behaviors: After the change, the hover popup shows only the definition text, and the visible identifier/marker (“p”) shown in the previous build is missing.

bug_report.summary: Rendered preview shows an extra empty block after footnote definition when footnote body contains an indented line
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Enter the provided content:
Outer reference [^outer]

[^outer]: Body text that includes an inner footnote-like reference [^inner] and an inline footnote-like pattern ^[not real nesting].
    Also include an inner definition-like line: [^inner]: should stay literal text.
Wait for preview/syntax highlighting to update (Rendering: Preview).
expected_behaviors: Footnote definition for [^outer] renders with its body content only (including the indented line as part of the footnote body).
No additional empty paragraph/block is rendered after the footnote body.
actual_behaviors: An extra empty block/paragraph is rendered directly underneath the footnote body in the preview (visible as a blank, outlined region under the footnote content).

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Preview renders an unexpected empty paragraph/block before the first line after adding text after footnotes
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Enter the scenario content:
Outer reference [^outer]

[^outer]: Body text that includes an inner footnote-like reference [^inner] and an inline footnote-like pattern ^[not real nesting].
    Also include an inner definition-like line: [^inner]: should stay literal text.

Then append at the end of the document:

More text after footnotes to confirm responsiveness.
Wait for the preview to update.
expected_behaviors: Preview should start directly with the rendered first line (“Outer reference …”), with no blank/empty block inserted above it.
actual_behaviors: Preview shows an extra empty paragraph/block (blank rendered region) above the first rendered line, pushing the content down.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Ctrl-click footnote reference shows large popover overlay (with Edit button) instead of the prior interaction; may block direct footnote editing
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown document containing a footnote reference like: `Outer reference [^outer]` and a corresponding definition.
In the preview/editor area, position the pointer over the `[^outer]` reference.
Hold Ctrl and click the `[^outer]` reference (per UI hint for editing/jumping).
expected_behaviors: Ctrl-click should open the footnote editing interaction directly (or at minimum not introduce a new large overlay that changes the interaction).
No large tooltip/popover should obscure the document unless explicitly invoked.
actual_behaviors: A large popover/tooltip appears over the document showing the footnote contents and an “Edit” button, obscuring the view and changing the interaction compared to before.

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch

bug_report.summary: Footnote ‘Edit’ action leaves large popup overlay open, obscuring the document while editing
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown document containing a footnote reference and definition (e.g., `Outer reference [^outer]` and `[^outer]: ...`).
Trigger the footnote popup (e.g., Ctrl-click or otherwise open the footnote preview popup).
Click the popup’s “Edit” button.
expected_behaviors: Entering footnote edit mode should dismiss/close the large popup overlay so the user can edit without the popup obscuring the document.
actual_behaviors: After clicking “Edit”, the large popup remains visible and continues to cover a significant portion of the editor/preview area.

bug_report.summary: Clicking footnote popup ‘Edit’ auto-selects footnote content and shows floating formatting toolbar (unexpected selection state)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown document containing a footnote reference and definition.
Open the footnote popup and click “Edit”.
expected_behaviors: Editing should place a caret/focus in the editable footnote content without automatically selecting large ranges of text unless the user selects it.
actual_behaviors: A large portion of the footnote content appears selected (UI indicates a non-trivial selection, and a floating formatting toolbar appears), suggesting the editor enters an unexpected pre-selected state.

bug_report.summary: Stray thin vertical line/artifact renders in right-side pane tab/status strip (near x≈895)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Zettlr main window in split view (default tutorial workspace is sufficient).
Ensure right-side panes are visible (top: “Split View”, bottom: another tab such as the newly created file).
Look at the tab/status strip area above the lower-right pane content (near the left edge of the pane content area).
expected_behaviors: Pane tab/status strip should render cleanly without stray 1–2px vertical marks/artifacts.
actual_behaviors: A thin vertical line/mark appears in the pane tab/status strip area (approx. bbox x=894–904, y=532–560).

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Deleting a footnote definition can leave an empty "[^fnX]:" stub line (fn2) after the refactor
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document.

Enter footnote references and definitions, e.g.:
Text with three footnotes: [^fn1] [^fn2] [^fn3]

[^fn1]: Alpha footnote.
[^fn2]: Beta footnote.
[^fn3]: Gamma footnote with a link to [GitHub](https://github.com/).
Place the caret in the "[^fn2]: Beta footnote." definition line and press Backspace repeatedly to delete it character-by-character until the line should be removed.
Observe the remaining footnote definitions in the editor/preview pane.
expected_behaviors: When the fn2 definition line is fully deleted, there should be no remaining "[^fn2]:" marker/stub line.
actual_behaviors: An empty fn2 definition stub remains visible as a standalone line ("[^fn2]:") even after deletion progresses to the point where the line should be removed (before-change does not show this stub; after-change does).

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch
